  - "meridian"
  - "priscilla"
  - "thema"
---
The Fault Line attempts to capture Marcus and Dae-Jung in downtown Los Angeles. What follows is a catastrophic, highly destructive chase. Operating completely off the leash, Marcus and Dae-Jung are in their element. Because Meridian has integrated its smart-city tech into LA's infrastructure, the city is a hacker's playground. Dae-Jung shatters structural supports to block roads, while Marcus hacks industrial chemical vents to flood intersections with localized smog, perfectly blinding Thema and neutralizing Priscilla's traction.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2027-08-12-2-2027-08-12"
parent: incident-on-2027-08-12-2027-08-12
title: "The Medusa Incident — A PR Nightmare"
tags:
  - "crane"
  - "dae-jung"
  - "hayes"
  - "marcus"
  - "serpent"
  - "the-serpent"
  - "thema"
  - "vivian"
  - "volkov"
---
Thema finally corners Marcus in a public plaza. Exhausted and furious, she demands he return to the team. Marcus refuses, brutally telling Thema that her entire 'safe resistance' persona is a corporate joke with no legs, and that she traded real justice for underwear billboards. The insult hits the deepest, most insecure part of Thema's psyche. She loses control. In the middle of downtown Los Angeles, with civilian cameras rolling, Thema unleashes her full Nephilim-class scale. She becomes an abominable, towering creature, the serpentine biology violently erupting from her body like Medusa. It is terrifying, monstrous, and an absolute PR nightmare. Watching the live feed, Vivian Hayes immediately calls the retrieval off, declaring that Marcus and Dae-Jung aren't worth the brand damage.

<!-- entry-break -->

Jess takes the highway to Fairmount in the spiral-era clothes — dark coat, boots. She is doing recon outside Clint's building. Maya comes out and asks if she's goth now. Jess explains the difference between goth and punk with complete sincerity. Maya says punk suits her, she looks like she has opinions. Jess feels genuinely bad. The operation is aborted. She goes home and eats Lina's pink food. Then Elena posts fourteen Life123 pictures. One of them is a grey flannel. The operations notebook opens.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2027-09-01-2-2027-09-01"
parent: incident-on-2027-09-01-2027-09-01
title: "The Arms"
tags:
  - "2027"
  - "ai"
  - "clint"
  - "elena"
  - "jess"
  - "key-event"
  - "lina"
  - "midnight"
  - "supernatural"
---
Clint wakes up twice in a week with Jess present in the room without being physically in the room — or not always physically. The locks are locked. She is just there in the way something can be present without being visible. He says her name quietly into the room each time. The sound stops. Elena hears her crying from somewhere. Lina has seen Jess levitate slightly in the kitchen while reading, coming back down when her name is said. She has appeared in doorways without using the door. Elena asks Lina directly at midnight: has Jess ever done anything you would describe as not normal. Lina says: I think she's magical, Elena.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2027-09-01-3-2027-09-01"
parent: incident-on-2027-09-01-2027-09-01
title: "Karen Models Once"
tags:
  - "ai"
  - "asphodel"
  - "karen"
  - "lina"
---
Seeking deep-state cover to obscure her intelligence profile, Karen Vance calls Lina and asks to model for ASPHODEL. She is assigned the scent *Wayward*. Shot in an undisclosed Mediterranean location by a photographer paid to forget his own name, Karen wears a dark coat and stares at the camera with the specific expression of someone who has already won the argument she is about to have with you. Chat notes she gives 'villain energy.'

<!-- entry-break -->

During a period of profound operational stress under Hillary's ongoing campaign, Elena Vasquez executes a series of strategic maneuvers against Jess and Clint's partnership. Operating on an available rather than correct arithmetic, she logs her moves under her internal "wrong but executed" file. 

During a subsequent confrontation at Smith's Ice Cream, Elena undergoes a total calculation stillness as Jess reveals her multi-year file on the original Ratgirlz rejection. Elena flatly reveals: *“I liked you. I liked you the whole time”*. The admission bridges the final gap in their tracking records, with Jess acknowledging the accuracy of the baseline connection.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "the-vasquez-offensive-2-2027-09-05"
parent: the-vasquez-offensive-2027-09-05
title: "The Vasquez Offensive — Elena Makes Her Move"
tags:
  - "ai"
  - "clint"
  - "elena"
  - "jess"
  - "neil"
  - "shadow-unit"
---
Sensing that Jess is completely destabilized by the engagement and Neil's new relationship, Elena Vasquez accelerates her psychological campaign. Elena begins making overt, calculated moves on Clint Harris during Shadow Unit operations. She does not do this because she loves Clint; she does it because she knows Jess is watching. Elena is deliberately trying to prove to Jess that the entire concept of 'loyalty' is a fragile civilian myth that breaks under operational pressure.

<!-- entry-break -->

Running the financial routing for their new syndicate, Marcus confronts Jess about the source of the money. He points out, entirely accurately, that Lucius Braun is a bioterrorist who is almost certainly connected to the technology behind Hale's new disposable monsters, and that the Westons are a legacy white supremacist family with a horrifying history. Jess tries to explain that she and Lina are actively working against their families' legacies. Trying to be helpful, Lina earnestly steps in. She promises Marcus she isn't racist like her father, and explains with complete, unironic sincerity that she actually thinks of herself as a Black woman. Jess physically facepalms. Lina genuinely thought it would help.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2027-09-08-2-2027-09-08"
parent: incident-on-2027-09-08-2027-09-08
title: "The Humiliation — Veritas Intervenes"
tags:
  - "jess"
  - "lina"
  - "marcus"
  - "veritas"
---
Veritas walks into the Weston estate drawing room to find her elite, radicalized West Coast hacker unconscious on the expensive rug, bleeding, having just been absolutely demolished by an archivist and a giggling heiress. It is the most absurd, utterly humiliating defeat of Marcus's life. Veritas physically drags him out of the room, coldly instructing Jess and Lina to behave because the syndicate cannot afford to lose members to friendly fire.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2027-09-08-3-2027-09-08"
parent: incident-on-2027-09-08-2027-09-08
title: "The Thrill — Jess Understands the Knight"
tags:
  - "clint"
  - "jess"
  - "karen"
  - "lina"
  - "marcus"
  - "spiral"
---
*Addendum to The Weston Estate Brawl:* When the adrenaline finally settles and Marcus is dragged out, Jess and Lina look at each other amidst the wrecked antique furniture. They do not feel guilty. Standing there with a bloodied baseball bat, Jess is suddenly exposed to the specific, undeniable rush that Clint and Karen have experienced for years. She finally understands what they meant by 'the thrill.' They realize, with terrifying clarity, that beating a radicalized hacker unconscious with a bat and reality-bending anomalies was incredibly fun. The spiral takes another profound step downward.

<!-- entry-break -->

Still riding the adrenaline high of beating Marcus unconscious, Jess and Lina decide to share the good news. They sneak into the Voss apartment through the window in the middle of the night. They find Clint asleep and bruised from a recent patrol. Lina helpfully fetches a cold pack, and Clint wakes up to a sudden, double-hug from the two girls. He is initially horrified by the home invasion, but the reaction softens because he genuinely misses them. Jess excitedly recounts their victory over the West Coast hacker. Stunned, Clint actually congratulates them, admitting he always hated seeing Marcus in those Hayes Adaptive underwear ads on pirated movie streaming sites. Lina is shocked the Azure Knight pirates movies; he wryly notes he isn't a *total* boyscout.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2027-09-09-2-2027-09-09"
parent: incident-on-2027-09-09-2027-09-09
title: "The Thief — Clint Sets a Boundary"
tags:
  - "ai"
  - "ashford"
  - "clint"
  - "elena"
  - "jess"
  - "lina"
  - "origin"
---
*Addendum to The Window Raid:* The warmth of the apartment break-in fades when Jess holds Clint and tells him she misses him. He gently tells her she needs to cool down and stop being so antagonistic toward Elena (who is sleeping in the other room). He reminds Jess that Elena is just a girl who made some bad decisions back in Ashford High. Jess stubbornly refuses the framing, flatly calling Elena a thief who stole him away. Clint sighs, says *'this is why'* they can't just be normal right now, and affectionately kisses both Jess and Lina on the forehead goodnight. Lina drives Jess back to the Weston estate in her pink Volkswagen ID. Buzz.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2027-09-09-3-2027-09-09"
parent: incident-on-2027-09-09-2027-09-09
title: "The Pink Glitter — Elena Makes Omelets"
tags:
  - "ai"
  - "clint"
  - "dr-braun"
  - "elena"
  - "hillary"
  - "jess"
  - "lina"
---
Elena sleeps through the entire break-in. In the morning, she is actively practicing how to cook a proper omelet for Clint when she spots a distinct trail of pink glitter on the apartment floor—the unmistakable residue of Lina Braun's expensive shampoo. She correctly identifies the evidence as 'another raid from the Boone household.' Clint eats his omelet and admits he doesn't mind the break-ins; it is currently the only way he can interact with Jess without Hillary Weston creating a massive, terrifying scene at the estate. Elena accepts this tactical reality with her usual flat pragmatism, but nods and calmly informs Clint that if she ever actually catches the girls climbing through her window, she is going to beat them up.

<!-- entry-break -->

Elena sees Jess standing outside Voss's apartment building at 11pm. Not waiting. Not leaving. Just standing on the sidewalk looking up at the window with the light. Jess is not tracking the time, which is not normal for her. Her eyes are doing something. Glowing is the accurate word. Elena stands at the end of the block for approximately ninety seconds watching her watch the window. Then she leaves. She has not told Clint. They are both filing it under: not yet.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2027-09-20-2-2027-09-20"
parent: incident-on-2027-09-20-2027-09-20
title: "The O.B.R.I.E.N. Meeting"
tags:
  - "ai"
  - "ari"
  - "clint"
  - "jess"
  - "key-event"
  - "lina"
  - "notebook"
  - "obrien"
  - "spiral"
---
Ari arranges it. O.B.R.I.E.N. headquarters, two chairs. Everyone has a file on Jess. She walks past them with the notebook. Clint is already there. She asks if she can sit closer. He nods. He holds her. She cries into his chest before she has said anything. He says: I think I will always love you. She asks as what. He says everything. Then he mentions that Lina kisses well and she slaps him, then laughs — the first real laugh in weeks. He tells her it's never too late to be good again. She says she will try. She meant it. He had been planning to come talk to her himself that day. The Weston estate gets there first.

<!-- entry-break -->

id: "the-weston-schism-1-2027-10-01"
parent: the-weston-schism-2027-10-01
title: "The Weston Schism — The Geneva Hotel Confrontation"
tags:
  - "hillary"
  - "lucius"
  - "schism"
  - "united-front"
---
Using United Front surface logistics traces, Hillary locates Lucius Weston’s Geneva hotel room and enters uninvited. Sitting across from him with absolute finality, she directly attacks his legacy: *"You took the most important inheritance our family ever received and you turned it into a pharmaceutical company. Dad gave us a war and you gave it an HR department"*. Lucius reviews her data points coldly, counter-arguing that Turner’s street war yielded zero progress and total legal liability, while his own biomedical activation programs achieved more in eight years than Dark Sun managed in eighty. 

Hillary accurately counters that his eugenics work is structurally divorced from its ideological origins, as Lucius honestly believes he is tracking neutral dimensional anomalies rather than building a master race. Lucius cuts off the conversation via a phone call, filing his sister under "known threat, stable trajectory" to be monitored through Ari Braun's channels, completely underestimating her lack of boundaries.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "the-weston-schism-2-2027-10-01"
parent: the-weston-schism-2027-10-01
title: "The Weston Schism — The Geneva Confrontation"
tags:
  - "hillary"
  - "lucius"
  - "schism"
  - "united-front"
---
Tracking her brother's movements through United Front surface logistics, Hillary Weston executes an unannounced entry into Lucius Weston’s Geneva hotel room. She flatly accuses him of cowardice, stating: *"You took the most important inheritance our family ever received and you turned it into a pharmaceutical company. Dad gave us a war and you gave it an HR department"*. Lucius dismisses the ideological critique using his standard data-tracking framework, counter-arguing that Turner’s legacy yielded thirty years of extreme legal liability and zero progress, while his own biomedical initiatives achieved more structural results in eight years than Dark Sun managed in eighty. 

Hillary accurately counters that his eugenics program is entirely divorced from its ideological origins, as Lucius blindly believes he is tracking neutral dimensional anomalies rather than building a master race. Lucius terminates the dialogue by executing an unrelated call on his phone, permanently archiving his sister as a stable, known threat to be monitored via Ari Braun’s deep channels. Hillary departs, vowing to complete the multiversal mission Turner could not execute.

<!-- entry-break -->

For over two years, Volt has actively trolled Vox's operations, showing up at incident sites and mocking Vox's serious revolutionary ideology online. Vox's comment sections are entirely overrun by Volt fan accounts. Humiliated and furious, Vox sets a public trap, intending to execute the teenager on camera as a brutal warning. The trap is a spectacle. The spectacle gives the Azure Knight exactly enough time to arrive. Clint breaches the site, tearing through the sonic arrays, and pulls a badly scraped, half-melted Volt out of the kill zone.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2027-10-12-2-2027-10-12"
parent: incident-on-2027-10-12-2027-10-12
title: "The Interview Request"
tags:
  - "arthur"
  - "clint"
  - "death"
  - "shadow-unit"
  - "vigilantes"
  - "volt"
---
*Addendum to The Spectacle:* Azure Knight sets Volt down in a safe alleyway. Her homemade gauntlets are smoking. She was thirty seconds away from a gruesome death. Unfazed, Daniela looks up at the towering, armored vigilante she has spent years documenting and asks, completely sincerely: 'Can I interview you now?' Clint stares at the teenager, recognizing the exact same reckless, desperate idealism he had when he first called Arthur Vance. He decides right there to bring her in, strictly to ensure she doesn't get herself killed.

<!-- entry-break -->

  - "ai"
  - "clint"
  - "death"
  - "jess"
  - "key-event"
  - "lina"
  - "robert"
  - "zack"
---
Car crash. Tuesday. Jess gets the call at 6:22am and writes the time in the notes app. She sits on the kitchen floor. Lina finds her. They sit without talking. The last words Jess said to him: you're the worst father in the world, Linda Boone would despise you. She said it because he told her she was using Lina for her own purposes, using Zack as a lapdog, making bad decisions — and he was right about all of it. She said the worst accurate thing she could find to a man who had been loyal to Linda's memory since 2020. The funeral is Thursday. Jess tries to give a speech. She cannot. She only cries. Clint speaks. He says he didn't know Robert well, but he raised Jess, and Jess is one of the best people he has ever met. He lifts her when she is down. The ice cream shop afterward. The boop. It is enough.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2027-10-12-2-2027-10-12"
parent: incident-on-2027-10-12-2027-10-12
title: "Elena's Text — The Escalation"
tags:
  - "elena"
  - "jess"
  - "key-event"
  - "spiral"
---
11:47pm. The night of the funeral. Elena sends Jess a text: her mom didn't love her, her dad despised her, Jess will be in the deepest pits of hell suffering for eternity because she's a demon, a fucking bitch. The text is mean at the worst possible moment. It is also — and Jess will document this later — kind of warranted given what Jess had been doing for months. Both things are in the permanent record. Elena chose the night of the funeral. This is what accelerates everything toward the Weston estate.

<!-- entry-break -->

Blanche arrives at Mia Henderson’s office to execute her plan but stops at the doorway. She observes the room: Mia in sweatpants, Clint Harris holding Jess Boone, and Lina Braun offering unconditional warmth. The sight of the "Evil Queen" in the role of a tired mother causes Blanche’s arithmetic to resolve in an unexpected direction: having killed Jack to save children, she realizes she cannot kill a mother. She permanently closes the operational file on the Ferrymen and enters the room as an ally.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-01-06-2-2028-01-06"
parent: incident-on-2028-01-06-2028-01-06
title: "The Kiss and the Threat Assessment"
tags:
  - "blanche"
  - "clint"
  - "jess"
  - "mia"
  - "whisper"
---
Inside the office, Blanche identifies Jess Boone from prior documentation and interrupts a silent exchange between Jess and Clint regarding Whisper. In a rare act of uncalibrated warmth, Blanche kisses Jess on the forehead, causing the chronicler to momentarily forget her notebook. Mia Henderson breaks into a rare, full laugh, while Clint Harris maintains a confused threat assessment. Blanche winks at Clint and tells him to "watch his back" before settling in to drink with Camila Torres.

<!-- entry-break -->

  - "elena"
  - "jess"
  - "spiral"
---
Elena Vasquez monitors Jess Boone’s ongoing spiral from a distance. Calculating that a direct, unconsented intervention will register as an attempt to overwrite Jess's independent internal methodology, Elena chooses to wait. 

Instead of delivering strategic advice, she sends Jess text messages on three separate Tuesday evenings containing plain observations, Argentine context, and a photo of a plant. Jess accepts the inputs, bypassing her rant notebooks to file the messages inside a separate section of Notebook 16 labeled simply: *“normal. filed as: normal”*—the highest structural designation in her taxonomy.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-01-15-2-2028-01-15"
parent: incident-on-2028-01-15-2028-01-15
title: "The Tether Holds — Jess and Clint Realign"
tags:
  - "ai"
  - "clint"
  - "elena"
  - "fairmount"
  - "jess"
  - "key-event"
  - "shadow-unit"
---
Elena Vasquez’s psychological siege ultimately fails. The Fairmount crashout of late 2027 pushes Jess Boone to the absolute edge of her architectural coping mechanisms, but it does not break her tether to Clint Harris. Clint, recognizing the operational and emotional sabotage, completely stonewalls Elena. He physically and operationally removes Elena from his proximity and spends January 2028 slowly, methodically rebuilding the baseline of trust with Jess. Clint proves that his pragmatism does not supersede his loyalty. The relationship stabilizes, not because the world is less terrifying, but because they have both finally accepted exactly how terrifying the world is.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
Realizing conventional tactics are failing, Ari authorizes Beatriz Santos (Blindspot) to leave her O.B.R.I.E.N. cell for the first time. The team tracks Luana to a safehouse. Bea stops time, casually walks past the perimeter defenses, and slaps Clint's ass purely to annoy Jess. 

Bea then walks into the safehouse bathroom where Luana is actively showering. In the frozen time, Bea grabs Luana, wraps a towel around her, throws a blindfold over Clint's eyes (and Jess's, just because), and physically drags Luana out of the safehouse and into O.B.R.I.E.N. custody before time resumes.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-01-18-2-2028-01-18"
parent: incident-on-2028-01-18-2028-01-18
title: "The Safehouse Debrief"
tags:
  - "ai"
  - "ari"
  - "bea"
  - "clint"
  - "hale"
  - "jess"
  - "luana"
  - "whisper"
---
Luana, wrapped only in a towel, finds herself surrounded by the Shadow Unit. Clint, standing awkwardly with a blindfold on, asks Jess if he can take it off. Jess tells him 'only after I do,' and then immediately tells him to keep it on anyway. Luana sighs and tells him he can look. Jess simply grabs Clint by the arm and guides him out of the room, blindfold still attached.

Ari demands to know what Luana is doing. Luana furiously explains she is done with Hale; she claims she saved Whisper from the airport because Hale was trying to kill them all. Ari softly corrects her: Hale wasn't hunting them. The airport security was chasing them because Whisper, an internationally wanted mercenary, casually walked through a civilian metal detector.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-01-18-3-2028-01-18"
parent: incident-on-2028-01-18-2028-01-18
title: "The Bird and the Blindspot"
tags:
  - "ai"
  - "ari"
  - "bea"
  - "blindspot"
  - "luana"
  - "whisper"
---
Luana drops the towel in pure, humiliated shock upon realizing her entire romantic outlaw escape was triggered by a TSA metal detector. Bea slow-claps, congratulating her on her elite tactical training. Ari notes that Luana's strategy needs work, but hands Luana the official 'Arara' combat suit anyway. 

Bea looks at Luana's eyes and realizes her best friend is fully committed to this life. Sighing deeply, Bea reaches into the O.B.R.I.E.N. duffel bag and begrudgingly puts on the hazard-yellow 'Ponto Cego' (Blindspot) costume. As the two girls stand together in full uniform, Whisper casually steps out from the corner of the room, noting he has been standing there the entire time. Bea looks him up and down and congratulates Luana on the massive upgrade. The team is officially assembled.

<!-- entry-break -->

---

Jess Boone learns of Captain Elias Harris's pregnancy misreading through a disclosure from Lina Braun. Jess sits in absolute silence for eleven seconds before logging the data in her notebook in small handwriting. She analyzes the configuration with flat efficiency, noting that by choosing a complex emotional evasion over a direct vigilante exposure, Maya has anchored the entire Harris family inside a permanent structural lie. 

Jess observes that Elena is now structurally stuck surviving a false narrative because explaining her actual unrequited Phoenix history to a career detective is logistically impossible. Jess instructs Lina to keep the discovery hidden from Clint, choosing to personally hold the file until the permanent record requires its chronological integration.

<!-- entry-break -->

Sitting in an O.B.R.I.E.N. safehouse debrief after extracting Luana from a shower, Beatriz quietly observes the dynamic between Clint Harris and Jess Boone. Watching Clint patiently observe Jess while she writes in her notebook, Beatriz runs the structural arithmetic of what it means to be seen and valued. Returning to her Asa Norte apartment, she stares at her phone for four minutes before opening her contacts.

Seeking neutral ground devoid of their past histories, Beatriz stops time and teleports Carlos to Christchurch, New Zealand at 7:14 PM local time. The teleportation serves as an accidental extraction, as hitmen seeking Carlos's architectural records arrive at his Asunción apartment forty seconds after Beatriz pulls him out. However, the date is immediately hindered by two critical logistical failures: Carlos does not speak English, and Beatriz forgot to exchange currencies for New Zealand Dollars.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-02-14-2-2028-02-14"
parent: incident-on-2028-02-14-2028-02-14
title: "The Translation Protocol — The Restaurant"
tags:
  - "beatriz-santos"
  - "carlos-ferreira-matos"
  - "humor"
  - "new-zealand"
---
Seated in a Christchurch restaurant, Beatriz stops time twice just to read the illegible, decorative font on the menu. They conduct their entire date via a translation app on Carlos's phone. When Beatriz apologizes for the disorganized nature of the date, Carlos translates that he has wanted to take her somewhere new since he was seventeen and does not require it to be organized. The app requests a user rating, which both of them ignore.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-02-14-3-2028-02-14"
parent: incident-on-2028-02-14-2028-02-14
title: "The Currencies Problem — The Escape"
tags:
  - "beatriz-santos"
  - "blindspot"
  - "carlos-ferreira-matos"
  - "fairmount"
---
When the bill arrives in New Zealand currency, Beatriz stares at it for four seconds before stopping time. Unhurried and accustomed to her logistical escapes, Carlos waits patiently with his fork halfway to his mouth. Unable to pay, Beatriz grabs his hand and teleports them away, accidentally miscalibrating her geographic anchor and landing them in a Fairmount, New Jersey hardware store parking lot at 11:00 AM on a Tuesday. Carlos observes the sky, confirms they didn't pay for the food, and asks if she enjoyed it; when she says yes, he declares they are okay.

<!-- entry-break -->

  - "history"
  - "key-event"
  - "leo"
  - "shadow-unit"
---
Elena (Shade) is running a solo, highly classified Shadow Unit infiltration on a Meridian logistics front. The operation is silent, surgical, and perfectly executed—until she drops into the server room and finds a twenty-three-year-old guy in a cheap jacket already there, frantically trying to download files onto a thumb drive. It is Leo Park. He has tripped three separate silent alarms. A Meridian kill-squad breaches the door. Elena does not have time to be annoyed. She deploys the darkness, violently grabbing the civilian and pulling him into the shadows.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-03-05-2-2028-03-05"
parent: incident-on-2028-03-05-2028-03-05
title: "The Flash Drive — Leo Does Not Run"
tags:
  - "elena"
  - "history"
  - "leo"
---
*Addendum to The Meridian Break-In:* Trapped in a corner covered by Elena's localized darkness, bullets tearing through the server room, Elena tells Leo to stay behind her so she can clear an exit. Leo is visibly terrified, sweating, and shaking—but he refuses to move until the download on his thumb drive hits 100%. He tells the lethal, shadow-wielding vigilante that he 'can't leave without the receipts.' Elena, accustomed to highly trained operators like Clint or Karen, is completely thrown by this ordinary, terrified civilian stubbornly risking his life for a flash drive. She finds it inexplicably charming. She covers him until the download finishes, then extracts them both.

<!-- entry-break -->

title: "The Gladiator Gambit — The Regent Captures Leo"
tags:
  - "history"
  - "key-event"
  - "leo"
  - "regent"
---
The Regent, the Order’s ruthless warden of living assets, intercepts Leo Park during his independent investigation. Categorizing him as 'high-quality livestock' with a unique lineage, she decides to bypass the standard silencing protocol. Instead, she prepares to move him to a secret containment site in Eden to serve as a 'gladiator'—a civilian body to be slaughtered in underground combat for the amusement of the Order's elite. Leo is physically outmatched and brutally handled, but he maintains his grip on his flash drive throughout the abduction.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-03-10-2-2028-03-10"
parent: incident-on-2028-03-10-2028-03-10
title: "Volt vs. The Warden — The Spectacle of Resistance"
tags:
  - "history"
  - "osha"
  - "regent"
  - "shadow-unit"
  - "volt"
---
Daniela Murphy (Volt) is the first to track the abduction. Operating on her trademark reckless idealism, she confronts The Regent alone, expecting a standard villainous encounter. She is met instead with one of the darkest, most clinical psychos in the Order's hierarchy. Through the comms, a panicked Osha Kelley—who knows the Regent's terrifying taxonomy firsthand—orders Volt to abandon the rescue and leave the civilian to die. Volt refuses. Utilizing her Osha-rebuilt gauntlets to their absolute structural limit, Volt puts up a fight that shocks the Shadow Unit when they finally arrive. Ari Braun notes the win with genuine surprise; the teenager successfully held the line against a warden.

<!-- entry-break -->

  - "elena"

  - "leo"
  - "shadow-unit"
  - "volt"
---
While Clint, Ghost, and Ari focus their combined tactical weight on restraining the captured Regent, Elena (Shade) and Volt breach the containment cell to find Leo. He has been partially drained of blood for the Order's alchemical processing and is in a state of profound shock. Horrified, the two women immediately extract him. Elena makes the executive decision to bypass Fairmount General, instead flying him directly to a high-security United Front medical facility under Lucius Weston’s personal jurisdiction.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-03-11-2-2028-03-11"
parent: incident-on-2028-03-11-2028-03-11
title: "The Blood Transaction — Off the Record"
tags:
  - "elena"
  - "history"
  - "leo"
  - "lucius"
  - "united-front"
---
At the United Front facility, Leo requires an immediate, massive transfusion. Elena Vasquez insists on being the donor, performing the blood transaction under Lucius Weston’s direct supervision. Lucius utilizes his organization’s significant administrative reach to scrub the incident entirely from the public and federal records. Elena stays next to Leo’s hospital bed for the remainder of the night, watching the color slowly return to the face of the man who risked everything for her uncle's receipts.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
When Leo wakes, he immediately apologizes to Elena, stating he should have put up a better fight against The Regent. Elena looks at him and sees the specific quality she first recognized in Clint Harris years prior: a complete, unyielding willingness to get beaten up for the right thing. She identifies it as her 'favorite type.' When she impulsively kisses him, Leo—already weakened by blood loss and overwhelmed by the shock of the evening—promptly faints.

<!-- entry-break -->

---

title: "The Monitoring — Lucius and Ari"
tags:
  - "ari"
  - "elena"
  - "history"
  - "jess"
  - "lucius"
---
*Addendum to The Hospital Kiss:* Watching the live security feed of the hospital room, Lucius Weston dryly remarks that the scene is 'cute.' Ari Braun, however, views the development through a purely strategic lens. She notes that Elena finding a new anchor is 'one less thing to worry about' regarding Jess Boone's territorial stability. Ari admits, in a rare moment of candor, that she finds Jess’s architectural volatility slightly frightening and prefers a configuration where Elena is no longer a romantic variable in Clint’s orbit.

title: "The Release — Karen Is Waiting"

  - "jess"
  - "karen"
  - "lina"
  - "lotus"
---
karen is waiting when saki is released. she has a photograph of jess sneezing on clint. she does not explain why she has this photograph. she hands it over as a welcome-back gift with the specific confidence of someone who has made a correct decision and is not requesting feedback on it. saki looks at it. she says: why. karen says: it gets funnier every time you look at it. saki looks at it again. she says: who is jess. karen says: you'll like her. she documents everything. saki looks at her. karen looks back. this is the first time they have been in the same room since october 2025. it takes place in a facility parking lot. it consists of a photograph of someone sneezing. it is sufficient. it is more than sufficient. *lina's note: karen brought a SNEEZE PHOTO as a welcome home gift. this is love. this is what love looks like in this universe. I'm framing it.*

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-03-15-2-2028-03-15"
parent: incident-on-2028-03-15-2028-03-15
title: "Release and The New Mission"
tags:
  - "ai"
  - "carmen"
  - "clint"
  - "fairmount"
  - "history"
  - "jess"
  - "karen"
  - "key-event"
  - "lotus"
  - "order"
  - "reunion"
---
Saki is released from the facility. Karen is waiting for her with a photo of Jess sneezing on Clint—a lighthearted target for their new life. Saki, now more famous than ever, joins the Fairmount group. The mission shifts: taking down the Order.

saki is released from the facility. karen is waiting for her with a photograph of jess sneezing on clint — a lighthearted target for their new life. saki, now more famous than ever, joins the fairmount group. the mission shifts: taking down the order. karen does a handstand in the hallway when the final holdout surrenders. she holds it for eight seconds. carmen reyes texts a pierogi recipe from wherever carmen reyes is at approximately the same moment. karen makes the pierogis. they are structurally inconsistent. saki eats one. she notes that it does not taste like her father's rice and that it tastes like something karen made and carmen would be proud of. she files both things in the permanent record.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-03-15-3-2028-03-15"
parent: incident-on-2028-03-15-2028-03-15
title: "Jess Finds the Atchison Schematics"
tags:
  - "2028"
  - "ai"
  - "clint"
  - "dorothy"
  - "engineering"
  - "fairmount"
  - "jess"
  - "key-event"
  - "liz"
  - "notebook"
  - "osha"
  - "roboter"
  - "writing"
---
In Osha Kelley's workshop beneath Fairmount, while Clint discusses equipment calibration with Osha, Jess finds a rolled document under the second workbench. The string holding it has gone brittle. It breaks when she touches it. The document unrolls across the floor. Ship schematics. Sheet three of four. Three annotation hands: Roboter's block letters, Osha's cramped technical script, and a third hand in ink that has gone brown at the edges. She knows it is Dorothy's handwriting before she reads it because she has seen it twice before — once on a card sent to Liz Hartley, once in a ship's log in Osha's locked cabinet. The third hand is hers. Dorothy writes in the engineering record: the kettle is a structural feature of this vessel. She writes: the ship knows. Jess does not cry immediately. She writes it in the notebook first. Then she cries. Clint comes and stands next to her. Osha goes still across the workshop. He says: she wrote those notes on a Tuesday. I know because she always wrote things on Tuesdays.

<!-- entry-break -->

---

  - "lina"

saki meets lina at the boone house to sign the ASPHODEL contract. she comes in. she looks at the kitchen. she looks at the kettle on the stove. she says: is that always on. lina says: it's structurally always on. saki says: my father used to keep the rice going the same way. lina says: we can make rice if you want. saki says: maybe next time. she signs the contract. she comes back the following thursday. lina has rice going on the stove. neither of them mentions that lina remembered. this is the most lina possible thing and it is in the permanent record. *lina's note: I remembered because she said next time and next time means she's coming back and if she's coming back the rice should be ready. this is basic hospitality. also I cried a little when she said it about her dad but I didn't make it a whole thing because it wasn't mine to make a whole thing of.*

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-04-00-2-2028-04-00"
parent: incident-on-2028-04-00-2028-04-00
title: "The Word Still Missing"
tags:
  - "ai"
  - "history"
  - "lotus"
  - "swan"
---
it is not gratitude. gratitude implies transaction and what clara did cannot be placed in a transactional frame. it is not debt, because debt implies it can eventually be repaid. it is not love exactly, though it is adjacent to love — specifically the love that is not romantic and not familial but is the third category, the love that arrives when someone makes an irreversible decision on your behalf without asking permission and then lives with it quietly and does not use it as leverage. the word exists. saki will find it eventually. it is in the open files.

<!-- entry-break -->

  - "hillary"
  - "order"
  - "political-alliance"
---
Hillary Weston meets Mayor-elect Morgan Dred at a judicial reform fundraiser in Fairmount, investing her remaining inheritance into deep institutional proximity rather than further active field risks. Hillary recognizes Dred as a professional state actor who commands structural matches rather than burning down institutions. 

Between the salad and main courses, she hands him a sealed folder containing her complete, decade-long cross-referenced map matching Astrid’s Dark Sun intelligence against the Order’s covert surface movements. Dred analyzes the portfolio in his vehicle, calling her the following morning to establish a permanent political axis. Bypassing romance, they marry in November 2028 in a small, heavily photographed ceremony, united in their intent to capture the Order's infrastructure for conflicting theological reasons.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "the-dred-introduction-2-2028-04-01"
parent: the-dred-introduction-2028-04-01
title: "The Dred Introduction — The Sealed Folder"
tags:
  - "dred"
  - "hillary"
  - "order"
  - "political-alliance"
---
Hillary Weston meets Mayor-elect Morgan Dred at a judicial reform fundraiser in Fairmount. Having recently walked free from her federal bioweapon trial following the Crossroads Mall incident, Hillary seeks to invest her radioactive inheritance into direct institutional proximity rather than active field risks. Dred recognizes her as a highly structured asset who avoids public destruction by quietly capturing existing regulatory mechanisms. Between the salad and main courses, Hillary hands Dred a highly classified sealed folder containing a decade of private research mapping Astrid’s Dark Sun materials against the Order's documented surface movements. 

Reviewing the data in his vehicle, Dred calls her the following morning to establish a permanent political partnership. Bypassing romance, the alliance is forged on overlapping methodology and diverging theology: Dred intends to capture the Order's infrastructure to enforce cold, legitimate governance by powerful actors, while Hillary views the surface capture of the Order as the absolute mechanism by which her bloodline will claim its dimensional inheritance.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
Azure Knight (Clint) spots Dr. Braun in Fairmount and attacks him directly, entirely abandoning protocol to make the architect of the city's misery pay. Braun immediately deploys Goluh as a physical shield. Clint does not hesitate; he runs the massive golem over with his motorcycle, the Steed. The Steed sustains minor damage, but Goluh absorbs the crushing impact, stands back up, and continues functioning, producing a horrific, guttural sound that mimics a forgotten name.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "the-incident-stage-one-2-2028-04-10"
parent: the-incident-stage-one-2028-04-10
title: "The Incident: Stage One — Aiko in the Dark"
tags:
  - "ai"
  - "aiko"
  - "clint"
  - "dr-braun"
  - "ghosts"
---
*Addendum to The Incident:* To cover his escape from Azure Knight, Braun deploys Aiko into the city streets. The seven-foot entity rampages through the district, destroying lamp posts while singing a terrifying lullaby. Ghost (Gareth Ellis) intervenes, using his phasing to trick Aiko into descending into the city's sewer system. Once she is inside, Ghost seals the entrance. Trapped in the dark, Aiko's twisted child-logic takes over, and she begins weeping uncontrollably. Ghost reports the successful containment, though he is deeply unsettled by the sound of her crying.

<!-- entry-break -->

#### Proposed Consolidation Structure:
To consolidate these entries, copy and paste this formatted block back into your markdown file, replacing the old separate entries:

```yaml
---
id: "incident-on-2028-04-10-1-2028-04-10"
parent: incident-on-2028-04-10-2028-04-10
title: "The Uninvited Guests — Jess Permits the Dance"
tags:
  - "clint"
  - "history"
  - "jess"
  - "karen"
  - "shadow-unit"
---
Clint Harris and Karen Vance arrive at the gala uninvited, in civilian formalwear. They take the dance floor. This highly visible, incredibly reckless infiltration is sanctioned by Jess Boone, who is remotely monitoring the operation from Fairmount. Jess permitted the dance because Karen has been on excellent behavior, successfully extracting heavily encrypted photographic intelligence from Eden as a trade. Jess watches the feed with clinical precision, verifying the perimeter while letting the Azure Knight and Scarlet Shade enjoy three minutes of peace.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-04-10-2-2028-04-10"
parent: incident-on-2028-04-10-2028-04-10
title: "The Hair Gel Test — Thema Intervenes"
tags:
  - "clint"
  - "fault-line"
  - "history"
  - "karen"
  - "thema"
---
Thema, taking full advantage of the Vanguard League's open bar, is having the time of her life when she spots Clint and Karen on the dance floor. Holding a profound, chaotic affection for the Azure Knight since 2017, she walks directly up to the deadliest duo in Fairmount and pours her cocktail entirely over Clint's head. She does this explicitly to test if his famously rigid hair is held by gel. It is. Clint stands on the dance floor, dripping with alcohol, while Karen sighs deeply. The disruption shatters their cover entirely.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-04-10-3-2028-04-10"
parent: incident-on-2028-04-10-2028-04-10
title: "The Feast is Over — The Azure Knight Speaks"
tags:
  - "clint"
  - "history"
  - "karen"
  - "key-event"
  - "shadow-unit"
---
With Apex on the floor and the ballroom frozen in shock, Clint Harris steps onto the main stage. He takes the microphone from the podium. He drops the careful, diplomatic 'spreadsheet' persona entirely, tapping directly into the theatrical terror of the Azure Knight. He looks out over the crowd of corporate sell-outs, Order assets, and corrupted vigilantes. His voice echoes through the gala: *"Ladies. Gentlemen. You have eaten well. You've eaten the people's hope. Their spirit. Your feast is nearly over. From this moment on... none of you are safe."* Karen immediately cuts the building's main power grid.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-04-10-4-2028-04-10"
parent: incident-on-2028-04-10-2028-04-10
title: "The Mother's Dance — Wraith and the Knight"
tags:
  - "clint"
  - "history"
  - "karen"
  - "key-event"
  - "volkov"
---
Before the lights go out, Elena Volkov (Wraith) steps onto the gala floor. She did not attend to manage the League; she came to check on her biological daughter, Karen Vance. Recognizing the Azure Knight, Volkov intercepts Clint and forces him into a slow dance. Moving with lethal, untouchable grace, she leans in and weaponizes his deepest trauma. She whispers: *"Patricia Voss was a tourist, little bird. She didn't know how to keep a pet. I would have built you a much better cage."* From the perimeter, Karen and Jess watch the exchange, experiencing a sickening, paralyzing mixture of absolute terror and violent jealousy. Volkov smiles and melts back into the crowd before the power drops.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-04-10-5-2028-04-10"
parent: incident-on-2028-04-10-2028-04-10
title: "Bloodline — Karen Confronts Volkov"
tags:
  - "history"
  - "karen"
  - "key-event"
  - "shadow-unit"
  - "volkov"
---
The Shadow Unit breaches the suite to find Volkov recovering. Karen steps to the front of the stack, drawing her blades. The genetic truth of her parentage—the absolute apex predator biology inherited from the Order's deadliest assassin—is fully unmasked. Karen does not flinch. She looks at her biological mother and delivers a string of terrifying, grimey psycho lines with absolute sincerity. She says: *"Hello, mother. I brought the coat-hanger you should've used. I'm going to peel your face off and wear it to my baby shower."* Volkov looks at her daughter, profoundly amused, preparing to draw her own weapons.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-04-10-6-2028-04-10"
parent: incident-on-2028-04-10-2028-04-10
title: "The Mecha Breach — Rin Interrupts"
tags:
  - "history"
  - "rin"
  - "shadow-unit"
  - "volkov"
---
Before Karen and Volkov can engage, the reinforced glass wall of the executive suite violently explodes inward. Rin Hasegawa, piloting her Fairmount-forged, brutalist MSY mecha, completely ignores the dialogue and the family drama. She simply initiates an all-out brawl, swinging the heavy Pangean alloy fists directly at Volkov's head. The executive suite disintegrates into a chaotic, multi-faction bloodbath.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-04-10-7-2028-04-10"
parent: incident-on-2028-04-10-2028-04-10
title: "The Limbo Drop and the Execution"
tags:
  - "clint"
  - "history"
  - "karen"
  - "key-event"
  - "shi"
  - "volkov"
---
Capitalizing on Volkov's moment of pain, Karen and the Shi rip open a portal to Limbo and violently tackle both Volkov and Clint into the dimension. In the chaotic void of Limbo, Volkov's sociopathic rage completely takes over. Ignoring Karen and the Shi, she focuses entirely on Clint. Moving too fast for anyone to intercept, Volkov brutally rips Clint's arm from its socket. Laughing maniacally, she slaughters him in the void. Karen screams, fighting desperately to reach him, but she is physically incapable of matching a Nephilim in Limbo. As Clint dies, his final, fading thought is not of his legacy or the war—it is simply the devastating image of Karen crying.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-04-10-8-2028-04-10"
parent: incident-on-2028-04-10-2028-04-10
title: "The Naked Return"
tags:
  - "clint"
  - "history"
  - "karen"
  - "shi"
---
Clint wakes up fully restored, but because his original suit was destroyed in the execution, he is completely naked. Roboter's presence fades as he opens his eyes. His very first emotion upon returning from the dead is profound, paralyzing embarrassment, as the entire Shi squad is staring at him. Saki blinks, looks at Karen, and bluntly asks: *"Did it always look like that?"* Karen, crying hysterically but fiercely proud, replies: *"Yes."* The Shi immediately start giggling despite the cosmic horror of the last five minutes. Limbo provides Clint with a new set of armor and the dormant, sleeping body of Tobias (a Midnight-Azure hybrid suit).

<!-- entry-break -->

---

---
id: "incident-on-2028-04-10-1-2028-04-10"
parent: incident-on-2028-04-10-2028-04-10
title: "The Rip-Off Aero — Clint Breaks Apex"
tags:
  - "apex"
  - "clint"
  - "history"
  - "key-event"
  - "vanguard-league"
---
Alerted by the commotion, the Vanguard League's security apparatus attempts to eject the uninvited vigilantes. Apex, the glowing 'Number One Hero,' squares up against Clint. Dripping with Thema's cocktail and profoundly unamused, Clint verbally dismantles the corporate symbol. He flatly calls Apex a cheap, engineered rip-off of Aero—reminding the room that Aero was not a hero, but a pathetic mass murderer. Apex attacks. Between his highly unstable Pangean steroid dependency and the alcohol in his system, Apex is exhausted within ninety seconds. Clint and Karen effortlessly tire him out, dropping Japan's greatest hero to the floor without drawing a weapon.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-04-10-2-2028-04-10"
parent: incident-on-2028-04-10-2028-04-10
title: "The Hostage and the Bite"
tags:
  - "bea"
  - "clint"
  - "history"
  - "key-event"
  - "volkov"
---
Amidst the mecha-brawl, Bea attempts to use her time-stop to land a lethal shot on Volkov. However, Volkov’s Nephilim-speed allows her to anticipate the spatial distortion. She dodges the bullets and takes Bea hostage, holding a blade to her throat and threatening to execute the unconscious Elena on the floor as well. Volkov demands Clint's surrender. Clint complies. Volkov forces him to unmask in front of the room. To establish absolute psychological dominance, she kisses him. Refusing to be entirely victimized, Clint violently bites down on her tongue, drawing blood and making her flinch.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-04-10-3-2028-04-10"
parent: incident-on-2028-04-10-2028-04-10
title: "Electronic Dreams — Roboter Fights Back"
tags:
  - "clint"
  - "history"
  - "key-event"
  - "limbo"
  - "roboter"
  - "watchers"
---
As Clint's life ends, the Watchers manifest in the Limbo dimension, their cosmic laughter echoing at the Azure Knight's brutal execution. Then, Limbo itself violently fights back. The Watchers are scattered—not by the Shi, but by the dormant, sprawling electronic dreams of Roboter. The ancient Pangean machine is inexplicably alive inside the dimension. Responding to the profound injustice of the death, Roboter intervenes. It reconstructs Clint's original body from the void. Simultaneously, drawing on Clint's dying thoughts and his subconscious desire to live a normal life without the burden of the armor, Roboter physically manifests his 'dream self,' creating Tobias (Midnight II) as a completely separate, physical entity.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-04-10-4-2028-04-10"
parent: incident-on-2028-04-10-2028-04-10
title: "The Lechun Hero Gala — The Masquerade"
tags:
  - "history"
  - "rin"
  - "vanguard-league"
---
To project global stability following their shadow-buyout, the Order of the King forces the Vanguard League to host a massive, international 'Hero Gala' in Lechun. The event is a grotesque display of corporate excess. Rin Hasegawa, currently operating off-the-books for MSY, infiltrates the gala in civilian clothing. To maintain her cover and avoid triggering an immediate firefight, she accepts a dance with Kenji (Kamen-Zero). They smile flawlessly for the flashing cameras, moving in perfect rhythm while privately despising each other—a perfect, silent encapsulation of the League's complete moral bankruptcy.

<!-- entry-break -->

id: "incident-on-2028-04-10-1-2028-04-10"
parent: incident-on-2028-04-10-2028-04-10
title: "The Fault Line Collision — Karen Stands Them Down"
tags:
  - "fault-line"
  - "history"
  - "karen"
  - "priscilla"
  - "thema"
  - "yoli"
---
As the brawl engulfs the building, Veritas, Marcus, and Rin Hasegawa converge on the floor. They run directly into the fractured remnants of the Fault Line—Thema, Priscilla, and Yoli. Disoriented by the blackout and lacking the geopolitical context, the Fault Line instinctively moves to defend the Vanguard League, not realizing they are shielding Order of the King assets. Before Veritas can escalate to lethal violence against her own sister, Karen Vance steps between the factions. Utilizing the absolute, terrifying gravity she commands, Karen orders Thema to stand down and look at who she is actually protecting. The Fault Line drops their hands.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-04-10-2-2028-04-10"
parent: incident-on-2028-04-10-2028-04-10
title: "The Underwear Spy — Marcus Fails"
tags:
  - "fault-line"
  - "history"
  - "marcus"
  - "veritas"
---
Veritas infiltrates the gala to determine the Order's global infrastructure, bringing Marcus to act as her primary espionage operative. Marcus attempts to charm information out of several international heroes, leaning into his new, radicalized, lethal hacker persona. The espionage attempt fails catastrophically. None of the international heroes recognize him as a threat; they only recognize him as the guy from the Hayes Adaptive boxer-brief billboards. Completely humiliated and entirely ineffective, Marcus is forced to retreat. Watching from the shadows, Veritas quietly updates her assessment of Marcus's field utility.

<!-- entry-break -->

Following her release from the facility, Saki Nonomiya accepts an open offer from Lina to front the perfume *First Breath*. Meeting at the Boone house for tea, Saki looks at PINKETTO and correctly identifies that the kettle is structurally sound. She signs a commercial contract designed by Ari Braun to function as a massive layer of public cover.

---
id: "incident-on-2028-04-10-2-2028-04-10"
parent: incident-on-2028-04-10-2028-04-10

  - "yael"
---
*Addendum to The Incident:* Ari Braun intercepts her father's escape vehicle on her motorcycle. Cornering him, she tells him he made a grave mistake leaving his office and must face the consequences. Braun looks at his daughter and coldly replies that his greatest action was marrying Yael and having Ari and Lina. The psychological strike is devastatingly effective, stalling Ari just long enough for his ultimate distraction to deploy.

<!-- entry-break -->

id: "incident-on-2028-04-11-1-2028-04-11"
parent: incident-on-2028-04-11-2028-04-11
title: "The Allergy to Clothes — The Skanks"
tags:
  - "cass"
  - "clint"
  - "elena"
  - "history"
  - "jess"
  - "karen"
---
The quiet of the recovery room is broken when Cass Thorne walks in, entirely amused. She explains the noise from the main workshop: Tobias—the manifested dream-clone—is currently suffering from an acute 'allergy to clothes,' and the women of the unit are finding it highly entertaining. Elena Vasquez (Shade) leans in the doorway, smirking, noting she will always love the original more, but it is certainly interesting to look at the duplicate. Karen fiercely defends the clone, stating it is literally just Clint's restored physical form, nothing more. Deeply territorial, Jess tells Elena and the others to scram, loudly declaring that the original Clint is hers and banishing the 'skanks' from the room. Clint pulls the blanket over his face in profound embarrassment.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-04-11-2-2028-04-11"
parent: incident-on-2028-04-11-2028-04-11
title: "The Recovery Room — Missing the Scars"
tags:
  - "clint"
  - "history"
  - "jess"
  - "shadow-unit"
---
Back at Osha Kelley's hideout, the adrenaline crashes. Clint is recovering in a cot in the back room, cuddling with Jess in a rare moment of quiet intimacy. He asks where the rest of the unit went. Jess ignores the question, instead asking with careful curiosity if the new, Roboter-forged body is better, and specifically if 'everything stayed.' Clint confirms everything is intact, but admits with genuine sadness that he misses his scars—the burns from Voss, the blade marks from the shadow war, and even the specific scar Jess gave him when she pinched him really hard during an argument. Jess immediately promises to give him new scars with 'wholesome meanings.' Clint goes completely rigid, horrified by the implications of the sentence, forcing Jess to hastily clarify that she just means making new, better memories.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-04-11-3-2028-04-11"
parent: incident-on-2028-04-11-2028-04-11
title: "The Mia Henderson Test"
tags:
  - "clint"
  - "history"
  - "key-event"
  - "tobias"
---
Operating as Tobias, Clint has to constantly fight through the cognitive fog of the clone's native instincts to remember who he actually is. He experiences the entire 'Tobias Fanclub' scene from the inside. He looks across the workshop and literally watches his original body standing in the doorway with Jess. To test if he is actually rewriting the physical past or simply hallucinating, Clint uses Tobias's hands to grab a pen from Osha's workbench. He writes his mother's name, 'Mia Henderson,' on a scrap of paper and slips it under a wrench. He then lets Tobias's hardwired instincts take over, perfectly executing the ballroom dip and kissing Karen.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-04-11-4-2028-04-11"
parent: incident-on-2028-04-11-2028-04-11
title: "Breaking Character — Karen's Reaction"
tags:
  - "clint"
  - "history"
  - "karen"
  - "tobias"
---
Karen Vance takes Tobias back to her apartment, completely overjoyed to finally have Clint's exact physical form entirely to herself without the operational baggage or Jess Boone's presence. Sitting in her living room, Clint fights through the dream-haze, breaks character, and tells her the truth. He explains that he is Clint's consciousness, actively dreaming and piloting Tobias's body. Karen processes this massive, cosmic reality-break for approximately three seconds. She smiles and says: *"That makes it better. Don't tell Jess."*

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-04-11-5-2028-04-11"
parent: incident-on-2028-04-11-2028-04-11
title: "The Escape and the Debrief"
tags:
  - "clint"
  - "history"
  - "jess"
  - "key-event"
  - "roboter"
  - "shadow-unit"
---
Carrying the sleeping Tobias, Clint steps out of the Limbo portal fully armored just as the Lechun police breach the executive suite. The entire Shadow Unit instantly extracts through the portal, returning to Osha Kelley's underground hideout in Fairmount. During the exhausted, deeply traumatized debrief, Clint explains exactly what happened in the void. Jess Boone sits perfectly still, extremely disturbed by the existential revelation that Fairmount's founding machine, Roboter, is functionally alive and dreaming inside Limbo.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-04-11-6-2028-04-11"
parent: incident-on-2028-04-11-2028-04-11
title: "The One and Only — Tobias Chooses"
tags:
  - "clint"
  - "history"
  - "jess"
  - "karen"
  - "key-event"
  - "tobias"
---
The crowd of operatives finally demands to know who Tobias actually likes. Because he is fundamentally Clint Harris's dream-self—manifested from the dying wish to just live a normal life with his family—the clone's devotion is structurally hardwired. Tobias does not hesitate. He announces to the room that he likes Karen Vance, the one and only. Moving with Clint's exact muscle memory, Tobias flawlessly executes Clint's signature ballroom dance routine with Karen, dipping her and kissing her in front of the entire workshop. Half the fanclub claps enthusiastically; the other half groans in disappointment and disperses. Watching from the doorway of the recovery room, Clint and Jess give the new couple a thumbs up.

<!-- entry-break -->

---

---
Clint's new Limbo-forged body comes with Vanguard's original enhancements, specifically localized gravity manipulation. Lina discovers this and dares him to pick up the TV remote using "the Force." Clint complains that he hates Star Wars and is a Star Trek puritan who prefers boring, structural logic. He attempts the telekinesis anyway. The remote lifts cleanly off the table, hovers, and then violently shatters into pieces. Jess is so shocked she spills coffee entirely down her shirt.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-04-15-2-2028-04-15"
parent: incident-on-2028-04-15-2028-04-15
title: "The Coffee Spill — Jess Feels Wanted"
tags:
  - "clint"
  - "history"
  - "jess"
  - "lina"
---
*Addendum to The Gravity Anomaly:* Jess takes off her ruined shirt. Lina, operating on pure, feral Ratgirlz logic, immediately starts cleaning the spilled coffee off Jess with her tongue. Clint casually states he will help, but makes absolutely no move to do so, simply for the record. Instead, he tells Jess she looks incredibly good and should stay in her undergarments for a while. Jess blushes deeply. Despite the sheer absurdity of the room, she smiles, feeling profoundly and completely wanted by both her spouse and her best friend. She stays like that for several minutes before the combined, undisguised admiration from both Clint and Lina makes her too embarrassed to continue.

<!-- entry-break -->

  - "clint"

  - "psychology"
  - "thema"
---
While the women of the unit are out on a mandated "girls' night," Thema visits an isolated Clint Harris. Clint confesses to suffering from profound imposter syndrome, struggling to match the architectural visions everyone has built of him. Drawing on her own history of adopting personas, Thema advises him that his true self isn't a vision, but a biological reality, instructing him to *"find your hands"*.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-05-11-2-2028-05-11"
parent: incident-on-2028-05-11-2028-05-11
title: "The Rotation and the Voss Request"
tags:
  - "clint"
  - "patricia"
  - "psychology"
  - "thema"
---
To provide Clint with an external perspective on his own orbit, Thema cycles through the physical forms of Karen, Jess, Lina, Elena, and Ashley, diagnosing their underlying psychological architectures. When Clint quietly asks her to shift into Patricia Voss so he can hear Voss say she is proud of him, Thema flatly refuses. She informs him that Voss forfeited the right to be proud of him, forcing Clint to accept that he must build his own validation. Instead, Thema shifts into Clint himself, allowing him to view his own fundamentally kind, decent face from the outside.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-05-11-3-2028-05-11"
parent: incident-on-2028-05-11-2028-05-11
title: "The Identification — Hayes Adaptive Sneakers"
tags:
  - "clint"
  - "humor"
  - "jess"
  - "karen"
  - "thema"
---
The women return to the apartment to find two identical Clints. Jess, Lina, and Elena assess the room, but Karen Vance solves the puzzle in three seconds. Karen points to the fake Clint on the couch and states, *"Hayes Adaptive sneakers"*. 

Thema shifts back, admitting that Vivian Hayes's highly engineered materials are beyond her biological replication capabilities. Jess logs the structural reality that the real Clint Harris is perpetually barefoot at home, finalizing the flawless identification. Clint proceeds to make omelets, and the evening settles into the warm, ambient noise of people safely inside each other's orbits.

<!-- entry-break -->

tags:
  - "ai"
  - "clint"
  - "dorothy"
  - "engineering"
  - "fairmount"
  - "history"
  - "jess"
  - "key-event"

---
Clint Harris and Osha Kelley recover the hull of the Atchison from the North Atlantic, sixty-two years after she was sunk. They transport the wreckage to a warehouse in Fairmount. Osha supervises the reassembly with the expression of a man receiving a letter he wrote sixty years ago. Jess Boone visits the ship a week later, sitting in Dorothy Kelley's common room chair and reading the engineering log she found in the workshop. The recovery marks a significant shift in the group's relationship with their history — the "Old Memories" are no longer just records; they are physical structures in the city.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-05-15-2-2028-05-15"
parent: incident-on-2028-05-15-2028-05-15
title: "Jess Sees the Atchison — The Kettle"
tags:
  - "2028"
  - "dorothy"
  - "jess"
  - "kettle"
  - "key-event"
  - "lina"
  - "notebook"
  - "osha"
---
Osha brings her when the reassembly is finished. She walks through the gondola with her notebook. She documents every room. The broadcast console where Dorothy sat. The phonograph shelf — empty now, the records long gone except the Crosby one in the box. The common room table with the scratch on the left side from 1960. The kettle bracket in the corner of the common room, where Dorothy's kettle sat for eight years. It is empty. Jess photographs it. She does not put a kettle there. She writes in the notebook: the bracket is there. the kettle traveled. it is in lina's kitchen. it is always on. it has always been on. it was on the atchison and it is on lina's stove and some things do not stop being structural just because the structure has changed. The entry is in notebook 24. It is in the permanent record.

<!-- entry-break -->

  - "ai"
  - "jess"
  - "karen"
  - "lotus"
---
jess boone asks saki in 2028 what it is like to be karen's operational pair. saki considers the question with the thoroughness she applies to everything. she says: she knows where the margin is. she doesn't know how she knows. she just adjusts. jess writes this down. she asks: do you think she knows about the forty minutes. saki says: she knows something. the knowing is precise enough that the number doesn't matter. jess writes this down too. she files it under: the form karen's attention takes. she sends it to no one. it belongs to saki and karen. it is only in the permanent record because jess puts everything in the permanent record and the permanent record gets the true things.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-06-00-2-2028-06-00"
parent: incident-on-2028-06-00-2028-06-00
title: "Saki and Clara — What They Are"
tags:
  - "ai"
  - "history"
  - "jess"
  - "lotus"
  - "swan"
---
they are not the same. they are the closest available approximation of someone who understands the same architecture. connected by a debt that has no currency, a blood tie that predates everything that happened, and the shared experience of the only dimension that runs on forty-minute margins. they make rice on thursdays when they are both in the same city. it still tastes wrong. they eat it anyway. this has been true since may 2024 and shows no sign of changing. jess boone files them in the permanent record under: a category the document does not have a clean name for yet. the entry stays open.

<!-- entry-break -->

Jess Boone records the final, permanent reclassification of Elena Vasquez in Notebook 22, twelve pages away from her Hillary Weston audit. She strips her original 2022 "temporary project" entry, documenting that Elena reads rooms with a survival-driven precision that captures reality rather than performance. 

Jess records that her own three-year resentment was an unworthy application of her tracking system. She places an asterisk at the bottom of the ledger: *“she was right that we were a bit strange. we are a bit strange. she fits anyway,”* permanently closing the file under the heading of essential assets.

They enter a new, unnamed professional register, operating as two people running adjacent algorithms around the same fixed point. Jess Boone notes the lack of incident in the June logs, filing it as a resolution reached through a "zombie-wedding film" and Karen Vance’s declarative truths.

Mayoral candidate Morgan Dred and DA Sinclair Aldrich finalize their political alliance. They do not cite the Voss case; they cite the dead FMPD officers and the 'execution' of Michael Aldrich. Dred calls Sinclair the 'Shield of Fairmount,' the man who will finish what the law was too weak to do in 2025. Sinclair believes Dred is the only politician with the spine to actually enforce the Accountability Act. Jess Boone notes: 'Sinclair is building the guillotine with honest hands; Dred is just waiting to pull the lever.'

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-06-12-2-2028-06-12"
parent: incident-on-2028-06-12-2028-06-12
title: "The Mordred Endorsement"
tags:
  - "ai"
  - "aldrich"
  - "fairmount"
  - "jess"
  - "morgan"
  - "order"
  - "sinclair"
---
As the 2028 Mayoral race intensifies, Sinclair Aldrich officially endorses Morgan Dred. Sinclair, who Michael Aldrich kept entirely shielded from the 'dirt' of O.B.R.I.E.N., believes he is endorsing a man who genuinely wants to close the gaps that vigilantism fills. For the public, Sinclair’s endorsement is the 'gold standard' of trust. For the Order, it is the final piece of institutional capture. Dred uses the endorsement to finalize his image as the restorer of the 'Round Table,' while Jess Boone observes from the periphery: 'The clean man is handing the keys to the traitor'.

<!-- entry-break -->

tags:
  - "2028"
  - "ai"

  - "elias"
  - "enhanced"
  - "fairmount"
  - "ghosts"
  - "key-event"
  - "vigilantes"
  - "vox"
---
an O.B.R.I.E.N.-flagged enhanced individual designated vox is operating out of a decommissioned radio tower on fairmount's western industrial perimeter. vox's ability is sonic — concentrated sound weapon capable of inducing neural disruption at close range. ana sokolov and elias harris respond to an incident at the tower. vox deploys the sonic weapon. elias is shielded by structural cover. ana takes the full burst. she goes down. elias cannot reach her — vox is between them and the weapon is still active. ghost comes in through the upper level. he incapacitates vox. he gets to ana. she is unconscious, pulse stable. he holds her hand. she is unconscious the whole time. he disappears before she wakes up. elias finds her sitting up, confused, saying: something — someone — was holding my hand. elias knows. he says nothing. he helps her up. he does not thank ghost. he never thanks vigilantes. he is aware that this is a choice he keeps making. he keeps making it.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-07-03-2-2028-07-03"
parent: incident-on-2028-07-03-2028-07-03
title: "The Radio Tower — The Hand"
tags:
  - "FMPD"
  - "ai"
  - "ana"
  - "clint"
  - "elias"
  - "fairmount"
  - "ghosts"
  - "history"
  - "jess"
  - "vox"
---
Vox's sonic weapon. Full burst, close range. She went down. Elias was behind structural cover; the weapon was between them and he could not reach her. She was unconscious the whole time.

She felt it anyway — not the hand in the way you feel a thing you know is happening, but in the way you feel a thing that has a specific weight you have felt before. She thought, from inside the unconscious: my father held my hand like this before a hard day. She was not fully awake when she thought it. She has not been able to confirm whether you can think things clearly from inside the unconscious. She was apparently able to. She has filed this under: information the body carries independently.

She surfaced. Gareth Ellis was gone. There was only the aftermath. She asked Elias who it was. He said: someone who was trying to help. She said: tell him thank you from me. He said he would pass it along. He did not pass it along. She found out eventually through Jess, who found out through Ana herself, which is the most circular intelligence loop in the permanent record. When Clint told her that Gareth had said she can keep it, she sat with this for a moment. Then she wrote in her private records: the kind of person who does the thing without needing to be known for it. This is a recognizable quality. I recognize it.

<!-- entry-break -->

---

```yaml
---
id: "incident-on-2028-08-15-1-2028-08-15"
parent: incident-on-2028-08-15-2028-08-15
title: "The FMPD Bullpen — Perkinson's Reality Check"
tags:
  - "ai"
  - "elias"
  - "mara"
  - "noah"
---
Elias Harris formally announces his engagement to Mara at the FMPD precinct. Detective Noah Perkinson, who had an affair with Mara years prior, is standing by the coffee machine when the announcement is made. Perkinson realizes his commanding officer is marrying the woman he used to sleep with, whose husband died while she was planning to confess the affair. The operational dynamic between the captain and the detective becomes instantly, permanently loaded with unspoken tension. Perkinson congratulates Elias with the exact enthusiasm of a man standing on a landmine.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-08-15-2-2028-08-15"
parent: incident-on-2028-08-15-2028-08-15
title: "The Shift — After the Tower"
tags:
  - "ai"
  - "ana"
  - "fairmount"
  - "ghosts"
  - "noah"
---
She does not announce it. It is simply that the next time she encounters Gareth Ellis in an operational context, she is slightly more present in the room than she was before.

He notices. He does not say anything. She does not say anything. The silence between them has a different quality than standard operational silence — it is not the silence of two people with nothing to say, but the silence of two people with something to say who have not yet decided to say it. Both of them are very busy. Both of them have been very busy for a long time. The being-busy is real and it is also, they are both aware, somewhat convenient.

Noah Perkinson notices from across the precinct. He manages to look simultaneously devastated and irritated in approximately equal measure, which is his most specific expression. He has had a feeling about Ana Sokolov since 2023, unspoken and entirely uncommunicated, and he is watching Gareth Ellis receive something he never asked for directly and therefore, by his own arithmetic, cannot complain about. He is correct that he cannot complain. He complains in the register of meticulous performance evaluations, where he begins filing exceptionally thorough reports about jurisdictional ambiguity in cases involving Ghost's phasing capability.

Ana reads the reports. She understands exactly what they are. She files her counter-reports with a thoroughness that suggests she is deeply invested in jurisdictional accuracy. She is deeply invested in jurisdictional accuracy. She is also conducting a cold war over a man who fixes water filtration in the mud and holds hands quietly and disappears before he needs to be thanked for it. Both things are simultaneously true.

<!-- entry-break -->

---

  - "HOT"

  - "lina"
  - "priscilla"
  - "thema"
  - "yoli"
---
Jess and Lina travel to Los Angeles to personally pitch the Heroes Outreach Team to the displaced Fault Line trio. Thema and Yoli immediately recognize Jess from the Fairmount gossip blogs as the Azure Knight's girlfriend. They dub her the 'lucky girl' and begin giggling among themselves. Jess is profoundly embarrassed. Lina, entirely misreading the social dynamics, decides to loudly defend Clint. She declares that there is nothing wrong with a man having a fat ass, that it is completely normal, and that they are weird for making it seem like a bad thing. Lina then shouts, *“Azure Knight has a fat ass!”* in the middle of the public venue. Jess is mortified as the entire room turns to look at them. Thema and Yoli burst out laughing and instantly declare they want Lina to be their handler.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-08-30-2-2028-08-30"
parent: incident-on-2028-08-30-2028-08-30
title: "Priscilla Declines — The Solo Attempt"
tags:
  - "HOT"
  - "history"
  - "jess"
  - "priscilla"
---
While Thema and Yoli are immediately won over by Lina's unhinged sincerity, Priscilla (Burnout) refuses the contract. Deeply traumatized by corporate handlers after her time with Vivian Hayes, she declares she is going solo. Jess respects the boundary and leaves the offer open. Over the next week, Priscilla discovers that without Vivian's corporate protection, the independent hero market is a nightmare. Because of her highly sexualized reputation from the Hayes Adaptive underwear billboards, the only private contracts she is offered are exceptionally creepy and inappropriate. Disgusted and out of options, she officially joins H.O.T. exactly seven days later.

<!-- entry-break -->

  - "history"
  - "jess"
  - "lina"
  - "priscilla"
---
The newly assembled trio needs an operational designation. Priscilla assumes they will simply use 'The Fault Line.' The United Front corporate lawyer shuts this down immediately, explaining the catastrophic legal and PR liabilities attached to the old name. Lina attempts to pitch 'The Runaways' and 'The Teen Titans,' blatantly ignoring copyright law and the fact that none of the women are teenagers. The pitches are aggressively rejected. They eventually settle on 'Vibecheck'—a terrible, profoundly lame name that somehow perfectly captures the team's energy.

<!-- entry-break -->

---
id: "incident-on-2028-09-08-2-2028-09-08"
parent: incident-on-2028-09-08-2028-09-08
title: "The Veritas Question — Jess Sets the Baseline"
tags:
  - "HOT"
  - "history"
  - "jess"
  - "priscilla"
  - "veritas"
---
*Addendum to The Naming Process:* Before signing the final United Front paperwork, Priscilla pulls Jess aside. She asks point-blank if H.O.T. associates with Veritas (Priscilla's lethal, estranged sister). Jess looks her in the eye and states firmly that she is not letting anyone who kills onto the team. Jess then pauses, visibly annoyed with herself, and admits: *'Well, except Bea. But I deeply regret that one.'* Priscilla genuinely appreciates the flat, unvarnished honesty. She tells Jess that she is already a massive improvement over Vivian Hayes.

<!-- entry-break -->

The mandated PR photoshoot takes place in a studio. Liz Hartley acts as the lead photographer. Clint is incredibly stiff. At the absolute last minute, Karen intentionally hijacks the tone of the shoot. While the Shi kneel around them holding roses in tactical gear, Karen forces Clint to carry her, aggressively kissing the Azure mask to claim visual dominance. Standing just off-camera, Jess watches her boyfriend's ex-girlfriend execute the romantic poses and is absolutely, silently livid for the entire duration of the shoot.

<!-- entry-break -->

---

  - "liz"
---
*Addendum to The Photoshoot:* After capturing the highly charged, borderline-inappropriate photographs of the Azure Knight and the Shi, Liz Hartley lowers her camera. She looks at the bizarre, heavily armed polycule and the intense jealousy radiating from Jess. Liz smiles and tells Jess that she absolutely loves this team because they are exactly as perverted and twisted as she is. Jess, still glaring at Karen, flatly replies: *"This is exactly why I chose you."*

<!-- entry-break -->

jess boone’s meticulous timeline calculations predict exact return date of smooth criminal. shadow unit prepares for standard kinetic heist. instead, criminal executes his ultimate theatrical masterpiece. utilizing stolen frequencies, he summons localized legion of limbo cryptids and unleashes surviving bio-zombies from dr. conrad braun’s abandoned laboratories. entities wander streets of fairmount while 'thriller' plays at deafening volume from every hijacked speaker system in city. despite facing literal apocalyptic horde, combined vigilante teams find scenario incredibly fun. combat is heavily choreographed by underlying bassline. only dissenting voice is director ari braun, who broadcasts over tactical comms that she absolutely hates song. entire united vigilante network, from ivory to vagabonds, unilaterally tells her to shut up.

---
caught in center of undead outbreak, jess boone and lina braun find themselves cornered at crossroads—geographic epicenter of fairmount's historical trauma. lina, solar deity capable of vaporizing city blocks, is completely paralyzed by her profound, documented phobia of zombies. jess boone, baseline archivist who forces azure knight to kill household cockroaches, steps in front of sun god. armed with aluminum baseball bat, jess systematically caves in skulls of advancing bio-corpses to beat of music. to her own absolute horror, jess realizes that she feels incredibly good doing it. lina remains cowering behind her, deeply traumatized by both zombies and jess's sudden, terrifying competence for blunt-force trauma.

<!-- entry-break -->
